abstract: This report analyzes evolving patterns of language use among the U.S. Hispanic population aged five and older from 1980 to the present. Spanning the fifty states and the District of Columbia, the study tracks transitions in household language preferences and the increasing share of the population demonstrating English proficiency. In addition to linguistic trends, this report provides comprehensive demographic and economic profiles of U.S. Hispanics, highlighting key longitudinal shifts in socioeconomic characteristics over the last four decades.
